title: Day 06 Interview Prep
description: Practice explaining custom hooks, debounce, responsive layout, and reusable architecture.

questions.question: What is a custom hook?
questions.sampleAnswer: A custom hook is a reusable function named useSomething that can call useState, useEffect, and other hooks. It extracts shared logic — debounce, theme, fetch — without duplicating code. Components call it like built-in hooks.

questions.question: What is debounce and why use it in search?
questions.sampleAnswer: Debounce delays execution until the user pauses — e.g. 300ms after last keystroke. Search sends one request instead of ten while typing. Throttle fires at most once per interval regardless of pause — different use case.

questions.question: How do you type a custom hook return value?
questions.sampleAnswer: I add explicit return type: function useDebounce<T>(value: T, delay: number): T. For multiple values I return { theme, toggleTheme } with a defined type or interface. Generics preserve input types through the hook.

questions.question: Custom hook vs utility function — decision criteria?
questions.sampleAnswer: If logic needs useState, useEffect, or context, it is a custom hook. Pure formatting or validation stays a plain function. useDebounce needs effects and timers — hook. formatDate is a util. Calling hooks from utils is invalid.

questions.question: How would you implement useDebounce?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Can they write the pattern verbally.
questions.sampleAnswer: I store debouncedValue in state. useEffect watches value: set timer to update debouncedValue after delay, return cleanup clearing timer. When value changes fast, previous timers cancel. Component reads debouncedValue for fetch or filter.

questions.question: How do layout components improve architecture?
questions.sampleAnswer: Layout components own persistent shell — sidebar, navbar, responsive breakpoints. Page components own content only. Router nests pages inside layout. Mobile toggle lives in layout, not duplicated per page. Theme and search in navbar compose hooks.

questions.question: useTheme stores preference in localStorage. What pitfalls do you handle?
keyPoints: Hydration mismatch if SSR — not our case today
Read storage in effect to avoid SSR issues
Sync DOM class when theme changes
questions.sampleAnswer: On mount I read localStorage in useEffect, default to light if missing or invalid. When theme changes I write storage and set data-theme on html. For SSR apps I defer storage read to avoid hydration mismatch — Vite SPA is simpler.

questions.question: Team has useDebounce, useDebouncedCallback, and useSearch in three files with overlapping timers. Refactor plan?
questions.sampleAnswer: I trace call sites. Core primitive is useDebounce(value, delay). useSearch becomes thin wrapper: debounced query + fetch effect. I deprecate duplicate timer logic, migrate one consumer per PR, add unit tests for timer cleanup. Document in hooks README.
